'''Paul White''' (born 7 December 1982) is an English-born former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]], and at club level for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]],<ref>{{cite news|title=Super League XI|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/4528920.stm|access-date=31 October 2022|work=BBC News|date=5 February 2006}}</ref> [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells), the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Keighley Cougars]], primarily as a {{rlp|wg}}, but also as a {{rlp|fb|so|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}}.<ref>{{cite news|title=White's Super Debut|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/whites-super-debut-5094587|access-date=31 October 2022|work=[[Huddersfield Daily Examiner]]|date=13 July 2013|orig-date=2 August 2003}}</ref>

In 2011, for the beginning of a rugby union season, Paul tried his hand at [[rugby union]] for [[Preston Grasshoppers]] scoring 6 tries in 6 games.<ref>{{cite news|title=Rugby Lions 24 Preston Grasshoppers|url=https://www.coventrytelegraph.net/sport/rugby/rugby-lions-24-preston-grasshoppers-3055069|access-date=31 October 2022|work=[[Coventry Telegraph]]|date=25 April 2013}}</ref>

== Representative career ==
Paul White played, and scored a try in [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]]'s 26–36 defeat by [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] in the [[2010 Atlantic Cup]] at [[Hodges Stadium]], [[Jacksonville, Florida]] on Tuesday 16 November 2010.<ref name="USA Beats Jamaica">{{cite web|url=http://www.wearerugby.com/news/articles/usa-beats-jamaica|title=USA Beats Jamaica|publisher=wearerugby.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>
